title: Opendoor Technologies Achieves Forward EBITDA Profitability
text: Key Milestone: Opendoor Technologies reported a breakthrough to adjusted EBITDA profitability on a 12 month go forward basis for the first time. This represents a significant operational turnaround for the iBuying platform. Operational Improvements: Management attributes the profitability achievement to several factors: stronger resale margins, accelerated inventory turnover, tighter cost management, and expanded artificial intelligence deployment. The company's new leadership under CEO Kaz Nejatian has emphasized operational discipline alongside technology enhancements. Mixed Financial Picture: Despite the forward profitability milestone, the company still faces challenges. In Q1 2026, Opendoor reported a net loss of $173 million on revenue of $720 million, compared with $1,153 million the prior year. The housing market environment remains pressured, yet the company is targeting breakeven adjusted EBITDA in Q2 2026 and profitability on an adjusted net income basis by year-end 2026. Investor Considerations: The stock has gained 16.9% over the past month and shows a 153.3% return over 3 years.
